response:
the polynomial function p(x) = -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 is used to model the engine's performance score. solving the equation p(x) = 50 involves finding the value of x that makes the expression equal to 50. this requires setting up and solving the equation -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 = 50. the equation simplifies to -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 70 = 0. this is a cubic equation, and solving it would require either factoring or using numerical methods to find the approximate value of x that satisfies the equation. the solution to this equation will give the number of years after servicing when the performance score reaches exactly 50.

the performance score p(x) must remain above 60 for the engine to perform optimally. this means solving the inequality -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 20 > 60. simplifying this inequality gives -2x^3 + 3x^2 + 36x - 80 > 0. this is a cubic inequality, and the solution involves finding the values of x for which the expression is greater than 0. the range of x values that satisfy this inequality will define the period during which the engine's performance remains above 60.
